name: System76 / Purism / StarLabs coreboot-based laptops
description: Independent Linux-focused laptop vendors (System76, Purism, Star Labs) ship coreboot UEFI firmware on their high-end models. CRTM_VERSION identifies the coreboot build + vendor-specific payload.

entries.notes: System76's 'Open Firmware' branding bundles coreboot + edk2 + System76-maintained ectool. TPM present on newer Oryx / Lemur / Thelio models.

entries.firmware-note: Purism Librem 14 ships Heads (coreboot + Linuxboot) as a security-focused boot option. Supports TPM-sealed LUKS and detached firmware signing.
entries.notes: Heads-based boot path produces EV_IPL events with coreboot-specific annotations.
StarLabs ships coreboot on most of their recent laptops. TPM 2.0 (Nuvoton NPCT7xx or similar).

notes: coreboot-based firmware has vastly fewer EV_EFI_* events than AMI/Insyde/EDK II because coreboot is purpose-specific and doesn't emulate the full UEFI boot service catalog. Verifiers should expect sparse PCR 2/3/4 state compared to mainstream UEFI hosts.
source: System76 Open Firmware docs, Purism Heads docs, StarLabs firmware release notes.
